subject: [PATCH] mm/shrinker: do not hold RCU lock in shrinker_debugfs_count_show()

2026-06-12.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the Linux kernel where the shrinker_debugfs_count_show function was holding an RCU lock while sleeping, which is not allowed. The function invokes the ->count_objects callback under rcu_read_lock, but this is unnecessary because mem_cgroup_iter takes care of RCU internally. The patch removes the unnecessary RCU lock and unlock calls, fixing the bug and preventing potential crashes. This fix addresses a problem that was introduced in commit 5035ebc644ae ('mm: shrinkers: introduce debugfs interface for memory shrinkers') and was reported by Zenghui Yu.
